title: Willson Contreras rides highs and lows of Red Sox win over Yankees
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a 2-run homer in the fifth, later expressing frustration with a Gatorade cooler in the eighth.
Contreras helped the Red Sox offense, finishing the game 2 for 4 with 3 RBIs against the Yankees.
Interim manager Chad Tracy praised Contreras's leadership, highlighting his 13 home runs and .299 batting average in 2026.

title: Severe thunderstorms could hit Mass. Saturday evening, forecasters say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Severe thunderstorms could hit Massachusetts Saturday afternoon and evening, bringing hail, strong winds, and heavy rain.
The National Weather Service warns areas west and northwest of Interstate 95 face the highest risk of severe weather.
Forecasters predict the highest chance of severe weather in Boston between 4 p.m. and 8 p.m., with conditions easing by 10 p.m.

title: Nantasket Beach stayed quiet despite fears of a social media-fueled ‘takeover’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Hull police increased staffing at Nantasket Beach after social media hinted at a potential 'takeover' event.
Authorities reported no incidents or disturbances at Nantasket Beach, noting a quieter-than-usual atmosphere on Friday.
State troopers suggested Carson Beach as the possible new site for the rumored gathering, but no incidents occurred there.

title: Red Sox’ Payton Tolle talks ‘playing with joy’ for self, team: ‘I try to come with a smile every day’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Payton Tolle made his MLB debut on August 29, 2025, striking out eight Pittsburgh Pirates over 5.1 innings.
Tolle uses on-field emotions strategically, inspired by his late mother's advice to 'play with joy'.
Tolle's lively personality and humorous postgame comments contrast with the struggling 2026 Red Sox team.

title: Colin Cowherd: Celtics future feels ‘weird’ compared to sustainable Knicks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Colin Cowherd argues Knicks have better long-term prospects due to Jalen Brunson's team-friendly contract.
Celtics' Tatum and Brown contracts take 70% of cap, limiting roster flexibility, according to Cowherd.
Knicks reached NBA Finals with balanced team play; Celtics exited playoffs early despite higher seed.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Hoppers' by Pixar is available on Disney+, featuring a teen using tech to communicate with animals.
'Pillion', a film exploring a BDSM relationship, streams on HBO Max without rating board submission.
'Cape Fear', now a series starring Amy Adams and Javier Bardem, is streaming on Apple TV.

title: Red Sox move Garrett Crochet to 60-day IL as ace continues to deal with discomfort
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox move Garrett Crochet to the 60-day IL, delaying his return until at least June 25.
Crochet's move allows Boston to add new reliever Joe La Sorsa to the 40-man roster.
Brayan Bello struggles lead to a demotion to Triple-A as Boston's rotation adapts without Crochet.

title: Endicott baseball falls to Denison in hard-fought DIII  National Championship Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Endicott College baseball lost the DIII National Championship 4-3 to Denison in an extra-innings Game 3.
The Gulls forced a decisive Game 3 by winning Game 2 with a 11-10 extra-inning walk-off victory.
Endicott's appearance marked the first time a school varsity team competed for a national title.

title: Laxative-laced brownies rattle Nantucket School Committee meeting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Nantucket resident Meghan Perry presented laxative-laced brownies at a School Committee meeting, critiquing PFAS in artificial turf.
The gesture targeted a debate over adding synthetic grass at Nantucket High School, highlighting PFAS contamination concerns.
No member consumed the brownies; police are documenting the incident, but charges are unclear under Massachusetts law.

title: Former McDonald’s manager accused of spitting in ex-girlfriend’s fries arraigned on felony charge
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kaylie Santos, 22, arraigned on felony charge for allegedly spitting in ex-girlfriend's fries at Southbridge McDonald's.
Recorded Snapchat video allegedly shows Santos contaminating fries while working as a night manager on April 9.
No other customers affected; McDonald’s owners cooperating with authorities to address the incident and ensure public safety.
